content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

`git diff` only contains the new bead entry in `.beads/issues.jsonl`. The task is directionally valid, but I found three correctness gaps in the description: it does not define how final-review quorum changes when a required reviewer becomes unavailable, it claims multi-stage coverage while omitting the existing completion/prompt-review runtime-failure paths, and its proposed text-pattern detection is broad enough to misclassify normal model output.

## Amendments

1. Update bead `ralph-burning-wt3` in `.beads/issues.jsonl:100` to explicitly define how final-review quorum changes when a reviewer backend becomes unavailable at runtime. The current code only skips failing reviewers when `member.required == false` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:225-235`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:427-437`) and still fails if executed reviewers/voters drop below `min_reviewers` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:281-286`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:473-478`). With the defaults, `DEFAULT_MIN_REVIEWERS` is 2 and both final-review backends are required (`src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs:25`, `src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs:1473-1477`), so the acceptance criterion to proceed with only one reviewer is impossible unless the bead also changes required-member handling and the effective minimum/consensus rules.
   Rationale: Without this, the task cannot satisfy its own acceptance criteria under the default configuration.
2. Expand the implementation scope described in `.beads/issues.jsonl:100` so “apply to all multi-backend stages” covers the actual runtime-failure paths outside final review. `completion` currently propagates the first invocation error from any completer and only checks `min_completers` after successful executions (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:109-182`), and `prompt_review` does the same for validator failures (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:165-237`). If the new failure class is only handled in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs`, the same persistent backend outage will still abort other multi-backend panels.
   Rationale: The new bead claims generic graceful degradation, but the listed code paths only address final review.
3. Tighten the backend-unavailable detection requirements in `.beads/issues.jsonl:100` so classification only inspects backend-generated error channels on failed invocations, not arbitrary stdout text. `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1478-1487` already treats stdout as an error only when the backend sets `is_error == true`; otherwise stdout can contain ordinary model output. Matching broad substrings like `billing` or `credits` across raw stdout/stderr would create false positives for normal task content.
   Rationale: Over-broad text matching can incorrectly classify healthy executions as infrastructure failures and drop usable backends.
